cards.back: This schedule mode allows tasks to be executed in parallel without any synchronization or communication between them.
This term refers to the parallel execution of tasks in the same thread, where each task executes independently, without any synchronization or communication between them.
The practice of using multiple processors or cores to execute multiple tasks simultaneously, improving the overall processing speed and efficiency.
Open Multi-Processing (OMP) is a standard for parallel programming in C/C++ that allows developers to write programs that can take advantage of multi-core processors and clusters.
The execution of multiple tasks or programs simultaneously on multiple processors or cores, which can improve the overall processing speed and efficiency of a system.
